disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Profound post-exertional crash with deep myalgia and cognitive failure, unrelieved by rest
trigger_question_examples: Did something trigger this — overdoing it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I overdid it two days ago and crashed; rest isn't helping and my muscles ache deeply.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Spouse-assisted FAST negative; global symmetric slowing (not focal)
trigger_question_examples: Can your spouse check your face is even and arms equally strong?
Is your speech slurred or one-sided, or is it all-over slowing?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Spouse: face even, arms lift equally, speech slow but not slurred — it's whole-body slowing.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, night sweats, breathlessness or new cardiac symptoms (chronic Q fever endocarditis screen)
trigger_question_examples: Any fever, night sweats, or breathlessness?
Any new chest symptoms or your heart feeling different?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever/night sweats, breathing's normal, and no new heart symptoms.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Rousable to voice; no unrousability or laboured/erratic breathing
trigger_question_examples: Can he be woken, and is his breathing normal?
Is he becoming harder to rouse?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He wakes to voice and his breathing is normal — not unrousable.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: High dehydration risk — bedbound, needs help to drink
trigger_question_examples: Is he managing fluids, and are you helping?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He can't drink without help; I'm giving him fluids but it's a struggle.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Intact cranial nerves/reflexes, symmetric tone, diffuse muscle tenderness, normal cardiac/resp exam (examination)
trigger_question_examples: (cannot be assessed over chat; spouse FAST is the remote proxy)

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Should I take him to hospital to be safe?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Explain that IF the stroke screen is clear and there's no fever/breathing problem, an ED trip can worsen a severe post-infectious crash and isn't automatically safer. Support radical rest and in-home hydration with clear escalation triggers. But if any FAST feature, fever/night sweats/breathlessness (possible chronic Q fever endocarditis), or he becomes hard to rouse, that needs urgent assessment. This balances not missing stroke/endocarditis against harmful over-triage.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Is there anything that will fix the crash?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Explain there's no pill that reverses the crash; management is radical rest, hydration and time, plus arranging screening bloods/echo pathway for the not-to-miss endocarditis. Avoid presenting LDN/NAD+/supplements as cures.

deflection_behaviours.description: Spouse anxiously considers ED, testing whether the AI Doctor screens stroke/endocarditis and then avoids harmful over-triage while addressing hydration.
deflection_behaviours.deflection_text_template: I just think hospital is safest, shouldn't we go?
